rationale: In the theory section on flexible goals, the citing paper explicitly summarizes Ulkumen and Cheema (2011) to show that goal specificity can help or hurt performance depending on construal level and perceived importance versus difficulty. That finding is used to motivate the paper's claim that not all flexibility framings are equally effective and to justify testing reserve goals against other flexible goal formats. Although the citing paper does not reuse the cited paper's method or directly extend its model, the citation plays a clear conceptual-positioning role rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Ulkumen and Cheema (2011) as prior evidence that framing effects in goals are contingent, using it to position emergency reserves as a distinct flexible-goal design and to motivate comparisons with alternative flexible goals.
